$comment: Allow-list gate for the repo ROOT and docs/. Enforced by scripts/check-manifest.mjs (`pnpm check:manifest`), wired into `pnpm gate` and the CI `verify` job (ADR-0022). Adding a NEW top-level root entry, or a NEW standalone doc under docs/, REQUIRES adding it here with a justification first — that friction is the point: it blocks temp/ad-hoc junk at root and keeps durable project knowledge in the Library (definition/principle/pattern/guardrail/techstack/adr/open-question), not loose prose docs. A new doc's justification must say why it does NOT belong in the Library. See the `repo-surface-allowlist` Library guardrail.

files.README.md: Project front-door (the one standalone narrative doc we keep).
files.tsconfig.base.json: Shared TS compiler options every package extends.
files.repo-manifest.json: This allow-list (self-listed so the check passes on itself).
files..env.example: Sample env (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); DB auth is keyless.
files..gcloudignore: Upload filter for `gcloud builds submit` (the hosted studio image, ADR-0042); must sit at the upload root, which is the repo root.
files.web: storytree-web (the public front-door site) vendored as a submodule; wired in .gitmodules (PR #111).

dirs.packages: Workspace packages (proof-protocol / storage-protocol / library / notice-board / studio-members / agent / orchestrator / drive / cli; @storytree/core dissolved per ADR-0068, @storytree/store dissolved per ADR-0077; the two root ports renamed for role-not-position per ADR-0078: verdict-contract→proof-protocol, base→storage-protocol; drive carved the build/orchestrate drivers out of cli so the studio drives builds without depending on cli).
dirs.docs: ADRs + open-questions backlog + research notes (see docs.* below).
dirs.stories: The story-tree seed: the work-hierarchy corpus, schema-validated by packages/library.
dirs.scripts: Repo-level maintenance scripts (e.g. this manifest check).
dirs.legacy: Read-only V1 Rust submodule (legacy/Agentic); reference only.
dirs..github: CI workflows (the green gate + auto-merge, ADR-0022).
dirs..cursor: Cursor project config; generated native subagents mirror the Library agent tier (ADR-0178).

allowedDirs.decisions: ADRs — immutable dated decision records; also the source-of-record the studio folds in as the Library's read-only `adr` category. New ADRs do not need a manifest entry.
allowedDirs.research: Long-form decision-provenance behind specific ADRs (option surveys / how a decision was reached). Does not fit the Library's atomic-unit model.
allowedDirs.friction-inbox: Offline/remote capture staging for `friction` Library items (ADR-0168 D2): a remote 443-only session that cannot reach the live store files `storytree friction new` here as validated doc JSON for its PR, and the adjudicator/librarian files it live and deletes the staging file (migrate-only, like sync-corpus). Schema-validated fail-closed by friction-inbox.test.ts in `pnpm -r test`. NOT durable knowledge — a transient staging surface, so it sits in docs/ rather than the Library.

files.open-questions.md: The deferred-decisions backlog, cited by stable section number (§n) from the ADRs and Library units' references. A threaded cross-reference ledger, not atomic Library units.

packageOwnership.$comment: The organism-boundary ownership map (ADR-0074, amended by ADR-0075), read by packages/cli/src/check-boundaries.ts (`pnpm check:boundaries`, wired into `pnpm gate` + the CI verify job). Every @storytree/* package under packages/* is classified EXACTLY once as an `organism` (owned by one story). The gate asserts every real runtime cross-organism dependency edge (each package.json `dependencies`; devDeps are test scaffolding and excluded, ADR-0010 §5) is COVERED by a declared cross-story edge — consumer-side in the consumer story's `depends_on`, OR provider-side in the provider story's `consumed_by` (stories/*/story.md, ADR-0074 §4) — so cross-organism coupling can't be added without a declared edge, and because the studio forest renders `depends_on`, every coupling is therefore UI-visible. NOTE (ADR-0075, 2026-06-19; the ports renamed for role-not-position per ADR-0078, 2026-06-20): the shared ports storage-protocol/proof-protocol are NO LONGER an exempt `substrate` class — they are ordinary ROOT organisms (stories/storage-protocol + stories/proof-protocol, depends_on [] / [proof-protocol]) that every consumer DECLARES depends_on, exactly like cli/store stopped being exempt 'composition roots' in ADR-0074 §2. They stay listed in `foundational` (a SUBSET of organisms, not a class) to carry ONE minimality rule the gate enforces — a foundational port may only depend on another foundational port — which keeps them zod-only / node+pg-free so the studio's browser bundle works. So a port dependency is a visible declared+rendered edge, not an invisible exemption.
packageOwnership.$comment_surfaces: Consuming SURFACES (ADR-0100): a surface name → the story that owns it. A surface is an apps/* app (the studio) — a sink that wires organisms together and is consumed by nothing. ADR-0100 brought apps/* into the same boundary scan as packages/*: a surface's package.json @storytree/* deps are COVERED by the same rule as an organism's (declared in the surface's own story depends_on), so the studio's real wiring is enforced + rendered, exactly the visibility-over-exemption call ADR-0074 §2 made for the cli/store hubs. A surface is never `foundational` and draws no inbound edge. (The public-website subrepo is also a consuming surface but ships no workspace package — its forest-world edge is a declared story node backed by the check:web-engine drift gate, ADR-0093, not a package scanned here.)
